# ONTOLOGY — Animalia

The categories used to attach canon facts, and the relation types used to connect them. Drawn from FOUNDATIONS §Ontology Categories and §Relation Types.

---


## Categories in Use

| Category | Notes for Animalia |
|---|---|
| **entity** | individual non-person beings (a particular feral aurochs, a notable artifact's nickname) |
| **species** | sentient peoples (humans, cat-folk, beaver-folk, mammoth-folk, basilisk-folk, etc.); also non-sentient fauna; <!-- added by CF-0035 --> artifact-mutated non-sentient fauna (CF-0035) as a distinct sub-category of non-sentient species, categorically distinct from Cluster D mythic-species sentients (DIS-3 firewall) |
| **person** | named individuals (initially empty at genesis; populated by later worldbuilding) |
| **faction** | bands, conspiracies, sectarian movements |
| **institution** | guilds, courts, temple orders, civic registries (see INSTITUTIONS.md); <!-- added by CF-0037 --> family-handled household-enterprise institutional forms (waystations per CF-0037) as composite institution + historical_process + social_role per CF-0021 / CF-0029 / CF-0034 / CF-0035 composite-pattern precedent — non-chartered family-line continuation distinct from chartered guild-bodies; <!-- added by CF-0038 --> canal-warden authority (Brinewick civic-watch subtype, tariff-and-traffic; chartered-watch derivative per CF-0038) distinct from ward-inspector (artifact-hazard; extraction-guild-chartered per SOC-4) — decomposition load-bearing in canal-heartland polities; lock-keeper guild (canal-engineering-adjacent; beaver-folk-concentrated per PEOPLES_AND_SPECIES); <!-- added by CF-0043 --> civic-chartered non-guild COMMERCIAL COMPANY form (Ash-Seal per CF-0043) as Brinewick-specific institutional anomaly — composite institution + social_role (technicians) + craft (containment expertise) + text_tradition (trade jargon, corporate mystique) + historical_process (company lifespan + defector diffusion) + faction (us-vs-them culture); categorically distinct from chartered guild-bodies (non-guild; civic-charter not guild-charter) and from family-handled household-enterprise (commercial corporate form with employees, not family-line continuation); operates under concordat with chartered-extraction-guild (guild retains extraction / ward-inscription / ward-inspection / chartered-brokerage per SOC-4; company absorbs containment / research / disposal); non-transferable Brinewick civic charter — NOT a replicable institutional template; <!-- added by CF-0046 --> regional institutional-cultural complex form (drylands caravan-corridor per CF-0046) as composite institution + route + ecological_system + text_tradition + social_role — corridor-as-node-network of wells, gate-stops, villages, caravanserais under caravan-rotation social form (CF-0024 substrate); institutionally thin with customary-corridor dispute resolution (caravan-master adjudication, corridor-internal only, not magistrate-reachable); reciprocity-primary economy with SOC-3 coin-at-edges; drought-cycle pulse shapes traffic/water-pressure; runner-reached village militia as informal emergency response (NOT CF-0020 subtype, NOT dragoon-precursor, NOT new charter); station-log oral-primary DIS-2-partial-literacy register in trade-tongue lingua-franca; categorically distinct from CF-0037 family-handled waystation tradition (water-node corridor-complex vs road-rest fixed-station; drylands-present vs drylands-structurally-absent), from CF-0024 standalone caravan-rotation frame (CF-0046 extends CF-0024 with institutional-complex superstructure), and from chartered polity forms (non-chartered regional equilibrium stabilized by geographic non-transportability of canal infrastructure) |
| **polity** | city-states, duchies, charter-towns, leagues |
| **place** | specific sites — a particular canal lock, a sealed quarter-town, a Maker substructure; <!-- added by CF-0038 --> named chartered cities with world-renown footprint (Brinewick per CF-0038 as composite `place` + `polity` + named-institution-bearing site) |
| **region** | climate-and-culture bands (Canal Heartland, Cold North, Drylands South, Fenlands West, Mountain East) |
| **route** | trade routes, canal corridors, bardic circuits, migration paths |
| **resource** | grain, peat, fish, copper, silver, ceramic clay, ward-grade pigment |
| **craft** | tanning, smithing, ward-inscription, containment-wrighting, bardry |
| **technology** | locks, weirs, qanat aqueducts, longsword smithing, wax-tablet recordkeeping |
| **magic practice** | (constrained) — only artifact-handling practices: extraction, containment, neutralization, brokerage |
| **belief** | doctrines, folk explanations, sect convictions |
| **ritual** | hearth prayer, charter-day procession, species-progenitor feast, ward inspection |
| **law** | civic codes, charter clauses, customary precedents |
| **taboo** | restricted speech (ward specifics), restricted possession (unregistered artifact), restricted intimacy (in some sectarian contexts) |
| **artifact** | Maker-Age devices, with class (inert / dangerous / catastrophic); <!-- added by CF-0040 --> mundane-tier subclass of "most inert junk" band (near-imperceptible-effect artifacts: breeze-dolls, tick-mirrors, weak coin-sorters, scar-rubbers — commonly found in canal silt, beach sand, peat cuttings, farming; CF-0040 inverse strength-rarity subset, NOT a new distribution tier, carries per-artifact CAU-1 contamination); <!-- added by CF-0041 --> Maker-sealed subclass (deliberately-entombed artifacts remaining inert until unsealed; CF-0041 sealed-inert mechanism; categorically distinct from CF-0029 guardian constructions per M-8 CH-0014 distinct-origin firewall); <!-- added by CF-0039 --> Maker-Age artifacts carry destruction-resistance as a material-physical property of the artifact-as-locus (CF-0039); modern crafter outputs (CF-0021) do NOT inherit destruction-resistance |
| **hazard** | ward-breach zones, contaminated water, marsh ague, winter fever, feral megafauna corridors; <!-- added by CF-0035 --> contaminated-fauna zones (wilderness-distal pockets where artifact-mutated non-sentient beasts persist, CF-0035) |
| **event** | discrete past or present occurrences (a particular ward breach, a charter signing, a plague year) |
| **historical process** | the Maker-Age fall, the Charter-Era integration, recent artifact-incident wave |
| **social role** | magistrate, ward-inspector, bard, herb-wife, scarred veteran, containment-wright, broker; <!-- added by CF-0037 --> stationkeeper (CF-0037) as inherited family-line life-path role distinct from chartered-trade or apprenticeship roles; <!-- added by CF-0043 --> Ash-Seal TECHNICIAN (CF-0043) as Brinewick commercial-company employee self-identifying role distinct from chartered-containment-wright (chartered-guild apprenticeship track) and from CF-0024 contractor-auxiliary (hazard-labor contractor) — combines craft-expertise register with corporate-mystique trade-jargon and fed-up-technician plain-speaking admission; defector-specialist as post-Ash-Seal portable-reputation INDIVIDUAL consultant role absorbed into existing M-7 containment-wright / occult-fallout sub-specialty holding clause (NOT a new M-7 cohort per Rule 3); <!-- added by CF-0047 --> drylands well-keeper (CF-0047) as named customary-authority mid-stratum lineage-inheritable household-enterprise social role — operates the water-node well-stop under drylands caravan-corridor complex (CF-0046 substrate), holds cross-species hospitality etiquette authority at well-approach, functions as CF-0044 verse-repertoire custodian (retiring-keeper-verse-book heirloom formalized), keeps the household station-log in DIS-2 trade-tongue as load-bearing competence; lineage-inheritable like CF-0037 stationkeeper (apprenticeship chore-from-young at 6-7 paralleling CF-0037) but categorically distinct from stationkeeper (water-node-anchored vs road-rest-anchored; drylands-present vs heartland/highland/mountain-east); mid-stratum working-texture reputation register without civic-registry backing (paralleling CF-0037 non-chartered posture); NOT a sixth M-7 cohort (Rule 3 absorption firewall per CH-0018 MYSTERY_RESERVE M-7 extension); NOT a new anomalous wage-class (absorbed within ECONOMY §Wage Spreads well-keeper household row paralleling CF-0037 stationkeeper-master) |
| **text/tradition** | charters, occult fragments, bardic cycles, civic registries, sectarian doctrines; <!-- added by CF-0046 --> drylands corridor station-log (CF-0046) as household-held informal corridor-commercial record kept by well-keepers in DIS-2 trade-tongue — sparse, oral-primary-backed, NOT an archival category (M-9 station-log-not-a-custody-channel firewall per CH-0018 MYSTERY_RESERVE M-9 extension; NOT eleventh/twelfth INSTITUTIONS §Recordkeeping stream per Rule 3 firewall); categorically distinct from tea-house-scribe contract-custody tradition (CF-0024/0028/0033/0034/0036/0044) which handles witness-binding contracts; the two coexist at caravan-corridor witnessing sites; trade-tongue corridor-application as DIS-2-convergence mundane commercial lingua-franca subset (NOT a distinct drylands-specific language per CH-0018 DIS-2 clarification); drylands oral-primary memorized-repertoire (long poems, riddles, caravan-tale, CF-0044 verse-turn hospitality register, verse-counter pedagogy) as the dominant-transmission register within which the station-log operates as light-notation supplement |
| **ecological system** | canal-river hydrology, fenland water-table, drylands aquifer, highland herd-cycle |
| **bodily condition** | species-typical embodiment effects, fever-state, contamination-injury, war-wound |
| **metaphysical rule** | the artifact-magic constraint itself; the no-interbreeding rule; the embodiment-of-sentience rule |

---

## Relation Types in Use

The relation types below are declared explicitly. Any canon fact that asserts a relationship between two entities should use one of these types in its `notes` or in derived-canon links, not free prose.

| Relation | Use in Animalia |
|---|---|
| **causes** | "Maker-Age fall causes presence of artifacts in modern soil" |
| **enables** | "canal network enables multispecies labor markets" |
| **constrains** | "winter fever constrains highland population" |
| **depends_on** | "containment depends_on ceramic vessels of specific make" |
| **monopolized_by** | "artifact extraction monopolized_by chartered guilds (with black-market exception)" |
| **feared_by** | "ward breach feared_by all settled communities" |
| **worshipped_by** | "species-progenitor figures worshipped_by their species' progenitor cults" |
| **forbidden_to** | "discussion of ward specifics forbidden_to non-guild members in public space" |
| **traded_by** | "artifact fragments traded_by chartered brokers and unlicensed smugglers" |
| **concealed_from** | "ward inscription patterns concealed_from public knowledge" |
| **descends_from** | "Charter-Era civic codes descend_from earlier fragmentary local customs" |
| **originates_in** | "all artifacts originate_in the Maker Age" |
| **destabilizes** | "recent artifact-incident wave destabilizes ward-economy financing" |
| **legitimizes** | "common-pantheon clergy legitimize civic charters in many polities" |
| **imitates** | "some sectarian rites imitate Maker-Age fragments without understanding them" |
| **corrupts** | "uncontained catastrophic artifacts corrupt local water, fauna, behavior" |
| **replaces** | "Charter-Era civic registry replaces older species-specific custom-courts in mixed cities" |
| **ritualizes** | "ward inspection cycles ritualize what is materially a hazard inspection" |
| **militarizes** | "frontier polities militarize their artifact-extraction guilds" |
| **mythologizes** | "bardic cycles mythologize Charter-Day events; civic archives keep the prosaic record" |

---

## Notes on Use

- A canon fact may attach to multiple categories (a ward-breach event is both **event** and **hazard**; a chartered guild is both **institution** and **faction** in some polities).
- Mythical-species sentients are **species**, not **artifact**, even though M-5 (Mystery Reserve) entertains the heretical possibility otherwise. The category attachment reflects what the world treats as true; mystery entries hold the disquieting alternatives.
- The **magic practice** category is intentionally narrow in Animalia — there is no spellcraft. All entries here are artifact-handling practices.
- <!-- added by CF-0035 --> Artifact-mutated non-sentient beasts (CF-0035) attach to **hazard** + **species** (non-sentient fauna sub-category) + optionally **local_anomaly** (for specific named zones) + **historical_process** (for the centuries-accumulated phenomenon). They do NOT attach to `person`, `faction`, or any sentient-entity category. They are categorically DISTINCT from Cluster D mythic-species sentient folk (DIS-3) and from Maker-Age guardian constructions (CF-0029; distinct-origin firewall). The surface-similar appearance (chimeric / anomalous morphology) must not collapse the taxonomy.
- <!-- added by CF-0039 --> Maker-Age artifact destruction-resistance (CF-0039) attaches to **metaphysical_rule** + **artifact** (as a property of the artifact-as-locus). Modern crafter outputs (CF-0021) do NOT inherit and remain destructible by ordinary means. Destruction-resistance is categorically DISTINCT from CF-0029 guardian-construction activation and CF-0041 sealed-inert mechanism; three Maker-origin phenomena must NOT be collapsed into a single craft-signature (M-8 CH-0014 distinct-origin firewall extended).
- <!-- added by CF-0040 --> Mundane-tier Maker-Age artifacts (CF-0040) attach to **artifact** (mundane-subclass) + **resource_distribution** + **hazard** (low-tier CAU-1 cost). NOT a new distribution tier — explicit naming of DIS-1 "most inert junk" band. Attach to **daily_routine** for ordinary-life encounter contexts.
- <!-- added by CF-0041 --> Maker-Age deliberate-burial / sealed-inert / "unsealing" convention (CF-0041) attaches to **historical_process** (Maker-Age practice) + **metaphysical_rule** (sealed-inert mechanism) + **taboo** (unsealing-dread idiom) + **architecture** (buried-building sites) + **text_tradition** (idiom transmission). Sealed-inert mechanism is categorically DISTINCT from CF-0029 guardian-construction activation (M-8 CH-0014 distinct-origin firewall); buried-building archaeology is NOT a seventh Maker-knowledge custody channel (M-9 CH-0014 anti-custody-channel firewall). Site-specific sealed-Maker-buildings may attach to **place** / **local_anomaly** for named excavations under narrow-per-site commitments.
- <!-- added by CF-0042 --> Artifact-as-status-object economy (CF-0042) attaches to **institution** (declared-inventory display regime, bodyguard commission, theft-ring response) + **social_role** (status-artifact-displaying noble, bodyguard-contractor) + **memory_and_myth** (bardic-circuit silenced-contractor subgenre absorption of unsealing-dread material; state-security-concealment diffuse-rumor register). NOT a new Charter-Era structural axis — tracked as FIFTH parallel sixth-axis latent-burden watch-item.
- <!-- added by CF-0043 --> Ash-Seal commercial-company Brinewick anomaly (CF-0043) attaches to **institution** (civic-chartered non-guild commercial corporate form, concordat-arranged with chartered-extraction-guild) + **social_role** (Ash-Seal technician; defector-specialist post-Ash-Seal INDIVIDUAL consultant) + **craft** (empirical containment expertise, CF-0006 family, ONT-2-compatible artifact-handling craft) + **text_tradition** (corporate trade-jargon "Class-C containment, moderate environmental spread"; mystique-as-marketing register; "hundreds of years" antiquity-claim corporate mythmaking; fed-up-technician plain-speaking admission) + **historical_process** (Charter-Era-consolidation / Incident-Wave-era company-founding; multi-generational defector-diffusion into chartered-extraction-guild labor pool heartland-wide; century-plus Brinewick precedent-lock) + **faction** (us-vs-them company culture; containment-wright-faction defected-masters inside Ash-Seal vs in-guild remainder). Categorically distinct from chartered guild-bodies (non-guild civic-chartered form); distinct from CF-0037 family-handled household-enterprise (commercial-employer corporate form); distinct from CF-0024 contractor-auxiliary stratum (salaried commercial employees under trade-secret covenant, not hazard-labor contractors). Trade-secret research archive attaches to **text_tradition** as ELEVENTH commercial-trade-secret custody register (categorically distinct from civic-registry's ten existing streams per INSTITUTIONS §Recordkeeping CH-0015; M-1/M-9 firewall load-bearing — empirical-containment-craft only, NOT Maker-cognitive content custody channel). Brinewick-local scope; Ash-Seal civic-charter NON-TRANSFERABLE — not a replicable institutional template.
- <!-- added by CF-0046 --> Drylands caravan-corridor institutional-cultural complex (CF-0046) attaches to **institution** (regional corridor-as-node-network of wells, gate-stops, villages, caravanserais under caravan-rotation social form per CF-0024; institutionally thin with customary-corridor adjudication) + **route** (tertiary caravan-road network per GEOGRAPHY; drought-cycle traffic-pulse) + **ecological_system** (drought-cycle water-discipline keying corridor traffic + life rhythm) + **text_tradition** (oral-primary DIS-2 partial-literacy register; station-log convention in trade-tongue corridor-lingua; verse-counter + memorized-repertoire pedagogy; CF-0044 verse-turn hospitality register as sub-component) + **historical_process** (pre-Charter folk-tradition residue; Charter-Era neither-chartered-nor-displaced paralleling CF-0037/CF-0044) + **law** (caravan-master CORRIDOR-INTERNAL customary-adjudication; multi-caravan-master reputation-boycott as brake; NOT magistrate-reachable, NOT extradition-carrying). Categorically distinct from CF-0037 family-handled waystation tradition (water-node corridor-complex vs road-rest fixed-station; drylands-present vs drylands-structurally-absent); extends CF-0024 caravan-rotation frame (institutional-complex superstructure added to the standalone rotation-substrate); operates under SOC-3 coin-at-edges preservation clarification (CH-0018). NOT a sixth Charter-Era polity-asymmetric structural axis — uncontested regional-consensus institutional-thinness pattern (parallel to CF-0037 and CF-0044 non-chartered positions). NOT a new INSTITUTIONS §Recordkeeping archival category (M-9 station-log-not-a-custody-channel firewall per CH-0018). NOT a new CF-0020 militia subtype (runner-reached village militia is informal emergency-response convention per CH-0018 INSTITUTIONS §Military/Defense firewall).
- <!-- added by CF-0047 --> Drylands well-keeper (CF-0047) attaches to **social_role** (named customary-authority mid-stratum life-path role) + **institution** (lineage-inheritable household-enterprise office operating the water-node well-stop under CF-0046 substrate; partible-with-inheritance-dispute paralleling CF-0037) + **craft** (hospitality-keeping, station-log keeping in trade-tongue, CF-0044 verse-repertoire custody — composite empirical-craft register). NOT a sixth M-7 oracle-reputation cohort (Rule 3 absorption firewall per CH-0018 MYSTERY_RESERVE M-7 extension; absorbed within existing tradesperson / caravan-master / CF-0017 circuit-bard / CF-0044 verse-competent-drylander register). NOT a new anomalous wage-class (absorbed within ECONOMY §Wage Spreads household-enterprise non-anomalous row paralleling CF-0037 stationkeeper-master). Well-stop tenancy / placement-right attaches to a FOURTH multi-generational tenancy / asset-class (see ECONOMY §Value Stores CH-0018 addition) categorically distinct from CF-0031 ledger-mark (negative-signaling civic-registry), CF-0032 subsection placement-right (skill-credential, mobile across reciprocal polities), and CF-0037 waystation placement-right (route-anchored, fixed-infrastructure, heartland/highland/mountain-east-present) — well-stop placement-right is WATER-NODE-ANCHORED, drought-volatile in asset-value, and customary-NOT-civic-registry-backed (drylands institutional thinness). Lineage-inheritance is institutional-role continuity, NOT knowledge-genealogy continuity (paralleling CH-0005 M-7 body-injury-vs-knowledge-genealogy discipline).
